program_name: Management for Energy and Environmental Transition (MEET)
admission_type: free access but requires passing a test (verification of personal preparation)

academic_requirements.summary: A recognised three-year bachelor's degree (or equivalent foreign qualification) is required. Holders of an Italian class L-18 (Economics and Business Administration) or L-33 (Economics) degree, or an equivalent qualification, satisfy the curricular requirements; if they also graduated with a final mark of at least 99/110 they are exempt from the verification of the adequacy of personal preparation. All other candidates must undergo the verification, structured into Module 1 (basic knowledge) and/or Module 2 (personal preparation).
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A three-year (first-cycle) bachelor's degree, or an equivalent recognised foreign qualification.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Curricular requirements are met directly by an Italian L-18 (Economics and Business Administration) or L-33 (Economics) degree, or an equivalent qualification. Candidates missing the required subject credits can acquire them through a 'bridging career' (percorso ponte) by enrolling in individual educational activities before applying.
academic_requirements.grade_threshold: Graduates of class L-18 or L-33 (or equivalent) with a final degree mark of 99/110 or higher (and English at level B2) are exempt from the verification of the adequacy of personal preparation. L-18/L-33 graduates with a mark below 99/110 must sit Module 2 only and pass it with a minimum of 18/30.
academic_requirements.test_for_other_classes: Candidates whose bachelor's degree is in a class other than L-18 or L-33 must sit two tests: Module 1 (verification of basic knowledge) and Module 2 (verification of personal preparation), with Module 2 passed at a minimum of 18/30.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign qualification apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation; a minimum CGPA of 75% is required and no remote interview/test is required for MEET during pre-selection.

accepted_proofs: A valid English-language certificate at level B2 or higher, issued no more than three years before the application.
For candidates without a certificate: an assessment via interview on the scheduled admission-test date (by contacting valutazionedeirequisiti@economia.unige.it).
For non-EU UniGeApply applicants: a certificate attesting that English was the medium of instruction (MOI) of the prior degree is accepted in lieu of a B2 certificate.
exemption_rules: There is no waiver of the B2 English requirement itself; candidates lacking a certificate are assessed by interview rather than being exempted.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency is checked at the personal-preparation verification stage (certificate or interview on the admission-test date); non-EU candidates upload the certificate/MOI evidence on UniGeApply for pre-evaluation.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of the adequacy of personal preparation (departmental admission test). NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants except L-18/L-33 (or equivalent) graduates with a final mark of at least 99/110 and English at B2, who are exempt.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Module 1 (verification of basic knowledge) for candidates from degree classes other than L-18/L-33; Module 2 (verification of personal preparation) for everyone not exempt. L-18/L-33 graduates with a mark below 99/110 sit Module 2 only.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] Scheduled on dedicated admission-test sessions set by the Department of Economics; exact dates for A.Y. 2026/2027 are not published as plain text on the admission page.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Module 2 must be passed with a minimum of 18/30. The programme is 'free access, but requires passing a test', so the test is a gate to enrolment rather than a competitive ranking.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026 (A.Y. 2026/2027 institution-wide window).
EU/resident applicants without a foreign qualification follow the standard UniGe pre-enrolment and personal-preparation verification calendar set by the Department of Economics.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: [uncertain] A precise programme-specific first-year EU application/enrolment deadline is not published as plain text on the corsi.unige.it page; the institution-wide spring master's window (closes 28 August 2026) applies.
application_deadline_eu.notes: No programme-specific early closure is recorded for MEET; the standard master's windows apply. EU/resident applicants are not ranked, but must pass the personal-preparation verification (Module 2 >= 18/30) unless exempt.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation outcome by 22 May 2026.
No programme-specific early closure is listed for MEET (unlike Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Maritime Science and Technology, and Computer Engineering bachelor).
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and apply for the study visa, then re-upload title and English/MOI documents at enrolment.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None recorded for MEET; the standard fall close of 20 March 2026 applies.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session. A 30 euro application fee applies for visa applicants (covering up to two course applications). Minimum CGPA 75%; no remote interview/test for MEET.

required_documents.notes: Information about educational background, additional courses, work activities, or internships. Uploaded on UniGeApply (PDF, max 2 MB).
Final degree certificate, or provisional certificate if not yet graduated. Uploaded on UniGeApply for pre-evaluation.
List of exams taken with number of credits and grades. Used to verify the 75% CGPA and curricular subject coverage.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency evidence (B2 certificate or MOI certificate)
required_documents.notes: A valid B2-or-higher English certificate (issued within the last three years) or, for non-EU UniGeApply applicants, a certificate attesting English as the medium of instruction (MOI). Candidates without a certificate may instead be assessed by interview on the admission-test date.
Passport mandatory for non-EU applicants (jpg format on UniGeApply); EU applicants may use a passport or national identity card.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa and re-upload title/language documents at enrolment.

source_quotes.quote: MANAGEMENT FOR ENERGY AND ENVIRONMENTAL TRANSITION (MEET); Master's degree; Class LM-77 R (Management); Language English; Genova; 120 CFU; 2 years.
This degree course is free access, but requires passing a test.

Candidates holding a degree in class L-18 or L-33 (or an equivalent degree) meet the curricular requirements and, with a final mark of 99/110 or higher, are exempt from the verification of the adequacy of personal preparation; others must take Module 1 (basic knowledge) and/or Module 2 (personal preparation), passing Module 2 with at least 18/30. A knowledge of English of at least level B2 is required.
Management for Energy and Environmental Transition (MEET): Minimum CGPA 75%; remote interview/test: No; additional interview after arrival: No.

Knowledge of the Italian language is not required for courses taught in English. The English language proficiency level is B2 or a certificate attesting that English has been the medium of instruction (MOI). Required documents: Curriculum vitae et studiorum; Bachelor's degree (final or provisional certificate); Transcript of records.
Fall session opens 26 November 2025, closes 20 March 2026 (outcome by 22 May 2026); spring session opens 9 April 2026, master's degrees close 28 August 2026.

uncertainty_notes: CONFIRMED: distinct English-taught master, not a track of another programme. Teaching language English, degree class LM-77 R, course code 11939, campus Genova, 120 CFU, A.Y. 2026/2027 (corsi.unige.it/en/corsi/11939).
The older parallel code 11427 is the same MEET programme but is being phased out ('in esaurimento'); the official page for 11427 redirects first-year applicants to the current code 11939.
Exact admission-test session dates for A.Y. 2026/2027 are not published as plain text on the admission page (entry_exam_or_test.dates marked uncertain).

A precise programme-specific first-year EU application/enrolment deadline is not published on corsi.unige.it; the institution-wide spring master's window (closes 28 August 2026) is used (application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline marked uncertain).
The programme is delivered 'mostly remote' per the official page; this affects delivery mode, not admission requirements.
